title: Identity information :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Identity information
We use biometric information and information matching to verify your identity and your right to New Zealand government services.
- Providing DNA test results You may be asked to have a DNA test to confirm a relationship when you apply for a visa. The test is optional.

## Providing DNA test results
You may be asked to have a DNA test to confirm a relationship when you apply for a visa. The test is optional.
- Collecting and using biometric information We use biometric information, like photographs, fingerprints and iris scans to verify your identity.

## Collecting and using biometric information
We use biometric information, like photographs, fingerprints and iris scans to verify your identity.
- Providing full birth certificates Find out what should be in a full birth certificate sent with a visa application. If you cannot get a full birth certificate, find out what supporting documents you can use instead.

## Providing full birth certificates
Find out what should be in a full birth certificate sent with a visa application. If you cannot get a full birth certificate, find out what supporting documents you can use instead.
- Providing your passport for an online application
